$comment: Ratchet manifest for the fixture-drift gate (scripts/check-fixture-drift.mjs), google-adk facet (the google-adk-ratchet task, 2026-07-03 — the third facet wired into the gate, after claude-agent-sdk + openai-agents). UNLIKE the other two facets, google-adk does NOT import its upstream SDK at runtime — `packages/google-adk/src/index.ts`'s `AdkEvent`/`AdkPart` are a HAND-TYPED PROJECTION of the verified wire (the file header cites google/adk-python's `Event`/`LlmResponse` classes + the Gemini `Content`/`Part` REST shapes; `@google/adk` is only an OPTIONAL peerDependency, never imported). So the 'inventory' this manifest ratchets is not 'does the facet's own import surface still match' (there is no such import) but 'does the INSTALLED reference SDKs' actual field vocabulary still match what the hand-typed contract assumes' — the two ground truths available in this workspace (RETARGETED 2026-07-08 to the OFFICIAL `@google/adk` 1.3.0: `eventField` now resolves from `@google/adk`'s split `dist/types/events/event.d.ts` + `dist/types/models/llm_response.d.ts`, and `partKind` from `@google/genai`'s `dist/genai.d.ts` — BOTH now direct `packages/e2e` devDependency pins, so the old `@google/genai`-via-`@iqai/adk` second hop is gone with `@iqai/adk` itself): (1) `partKind` — the Gemini `Part` interface's own field names, from `@google/genai`'s bundled `dist/genai.d.ts` (the part-kind vocabulary this facet's `driveAdkPart` if-chain switches on); (2) `eventField` — the `Event`/`LlmResponse` interface field names, from `@google/adk`'s split `dist/types/events/event.d.ts` + `dist/types/models/llm_response.d.ts` (`export interface Event extends LlmResponse`; both interfaces' OWN fields are flattened into one inventory, since the hand-typed `AdkEvent` interface itself makes no such split). Dispositions were seeded 2026-07-03 by reading `driveAdkPart`/`driveAdkTopLevel`/`maybeCloseTurn` line-by-line against every installed field. handled = the facet has a dedicated, correct branch (or is genuinely CONSUMED for correlation, e.g. `id` in `turnKey()`); carried = routed losslessly to the existing `provider-raw` content.block carry (mirrors `driveAdkTopLevel`'s pre-existing `unmappedActions`/`unmappedEvent` named-field-ledger pattern — NOT a generic reflect-over-keys catch-all); not-applicable = provably cannot be a meaningful JSON-wire value on this facet's ingestion boundary; silently-dropped = a genuine, disclosed gap left un-landed (see below). FIVE findings were closed by carries: on `partKind`, ADDING the unmapped-part-fields carry (a new, unconditional check at the TOP of `driveAdkPart`, checked BEFORE the kind-specific if-chain's early returns — necessary because `videoMetadata` normally rides ALONGSIDE an already-matched `inlineData`/`fileData` part per genai's own doc, so a check placed only in an 'else' fallback would never see it) closed `mediaResolution`/`videoMetadata`/`toolCall`/`toolResponse`/`partMetadata`; on `eventField`, extending the PRE-EXISTING `unmappedEvent` bag in `driveAdkTopLevel` closed `candidateIndex` (absent from the hand-typed contract entirely) and `branch` (already typed on `AdkEvent` but never read). TWO findings were seeded, investigated, then DELIBERATELY left `silently-dropped` rather than landed: `author`/`timestamp` are ALSO real, currently-unread `AdkEvent` fields, and on `@google/adk` 1.3.0 `timestamp` is REQUIRED (`timestamp: number`) while `author` is OPTIONAL (`author?: string`) but EMPIRICALLY present on EVERY captured event — empirically, folding them into the generic carry fired a provider-raw content.block on every single native event in packages/e2e's captured adk fixtures (`author:"spike"` is set on all events of both corpus scenarios), breaking the recorded golden `*.agjson.json` snapshots and the cross-framework convergence assertions; regenerating those cassette fixtures is outside this ratchet's boundary (facet + manifests + script + SPEC §8 only). See their own per-entry notes below and `index.test.ts`'s explicit negative-control test. SPEC §8 item 23 documents the FOUR carries that DID land (`candidateIndex`/`branch` + the five `partKind` fields). See .superpowers/sdd/adk-ratchet-report.md for the full rationale + wire citations.

partKind.$comment: The Gemini `Part` interface's own field names (`@google/genai`'s `dist/genai.d.ts`, `export declare interface Part { … }`) — the part-kind vocabulary `driveAdkPart` switches on. `thought`/`thoughtSignature` are MODIFIER fields (ride alongside `text`/`functionCall`/`functionResponse`, never a kind of their own), listed here as their own members because they are still distinct installed field names the gate must track.

text.note: Primary text content -> text.start/delta/end (with citations/providerMetadata.google.thoughtSignature when signed, audit M22/§8.8). When the SAME part also has `thought:true`, it routes to reasoning.* instead (see the `thought` entry) — `driveAdkPart` checks `thought` FIRST and returns before reaching the text branch, so a thought+text part is never double-emitted.

thought.note: Boolean modifier, checked FIRST in driveAdkPart's if-chain -> reasoning.start/delta/end + reasoning.opaque{kind:'signature'} when `thoughtSignature` is also set.

thoughtSignature.note: Per-part base64 replay signature (§8.8, echo-or-400). Rides FOUR places depending on which sibling field is set on the SAME part: reasoning.opaque (thought parts), text.start/end.providerMetadata.google.thoughtSignature (signed non-thought text, e.g. Google-Search-grounded turns), tool.args.assembled.signature (functionCall parts), and tool.done.providerMetadata.google.thoughtSignature (functionResponse parts).

functionCall.note: tool.start + tool.args.delta + tool.args.assembled. Null-id minting (audit M47, window-scoped multiset dedup for the partial:false aggregate re-send — see ToolCallMintState's file-header doc) when `functionCall.id` is absent, as it often is on the Developer API.

functionResponse.note: tool.done via functionResponseToAgBlocks() (MCP-resource-shape content[] passthrough, or a typed `data` block for a plain object result). Null-id correlation resolved by drive()'s event-scoped pre-pass (review finding c-i/c-ii); an ORPHAN response (no pending mint under that name) rides ext.google.unparsed rather than fabricating a dangling tool.done (finding c-iii).

inlineData.note: inlineDataBlock() routes by MIME prefix to the image/audio/file AgBlock arm (spec §2).

executableCode.note: -> a `code` content.block (codeLanguage() maps the closed LANGUAGE_UNSPECIFIED|PYTHON enum defensively to a free string).

mediaResolution.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet, google-adk-ratchet task): was NOT read anywhere in driveAdkPart — a genuinely-vanishing finding (Tenet-6). Now carried via the new unconditional unmapped-part-fields provider-raw content.block (checked at the TOP of driveAdkPart, before the kind-specific if-chain). SPEC §8 item 23.

videoMetadata.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet): per genai's own doc ('should only be specified while the video data is presented in inline_data or file_data'), this field normally rides ALONGSIDE an already-handled inlineData/fileData part — but driveAdkPart's if-chain RETURNS as soon as it matches the primary kind, so a naive 'else' fallback placed AFTER the if-chain would never see this sibling field on a part whose primary content WAS handled. Fixed by checking unconditionally BEFORE the if-chain instead (same fix as mediaResolution). Verified with a fixture carrying BOTH inlineData and videoMetadata on the same part (index.test.ts). SPEC §8 item 23.

toolCall.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet): 'Server-side tool call...the client is expected to echo this message back to the API' — a live-API/bidi construct, DISTINCT from the client-executed `functionCall` (echo-only semantics, no AgJSON tool-lifecycle concept models it). Carried via the same unmapped-part-fields provider-raw block rather than dropped.

toolResponse.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet): the client-supplied result of a server-side `toolCall` (paired with the entry above). Same carry.

partMetadata.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet): free-form per-part custom metadata ('name of a file/source...or a way to multiplex multiple Part streams' per genai's own doc). Same carry.

eventField.$comment: The `Event`/`LlmResponse` interface field names, from the OFFICIAL `@google/adk`'s split `.d.ts` files (`dist/types/events/event.d.ts`'s `export interface Event extends LlmResponse` + `dist/types/models/llm_response.d.ts`'s `export interface LlmResponse`). Retargeted 2026-07-08 from the old, unofficial `@iqai/adk` (a single `dist/index.d.ts` with both as `declare class`), when the capture agent + this ratchet migrated to `@google/adk` 1.3.0. Both interfaces' own fields are flattened into ONE inventory (17 from LlmResponse + 7 from Event; `id` is Event-only on `@google/adk`, so nothing dedups) — matching how the hand-typed `AdkEvent` interface itself makes no LlmResponse/Event split. Nested sub-object field names (e.g. AdkContent.role, EventActions' own members) are OUT OF SCOPE for this section — it covers only the TOP-LEVEL Event/LlmResponse field names, analogous to how the openai manifest's protocolItem section covers only top-level ModelItem discriminants, not fields nested inside a content/action sub-union. Migration diff vs the @iqai/adk inventory: REMOVED `candidateIndex` (a @google/genai GenerateContentResponse field, never on the ADK Event) + `error` (the @iqai Error-instance field, gone from @google/adk's LlmResponse which carries only the errorCode/errorMessage string pair); ADDED `citationMetadata`, `inputTranscription`, `outputTranscription`, `liveSessionResumptionUpdate`, `goAway`, `modelVersion`, `liveSessionId`.

id.note: Consumed as the turnKey() fallback (`ev.invocationId && ev.invocationId.length > 0 ? ev.invocationId : ev.id ?? "adk"`) when invocationId is absent — genuinely read, not merely typed.

content.note: content.parts[] fan-out is the CORE of the entire facet (driveAdkPart per-part dispatch).

groundingMetadata.note: mapGroundingCitations() (per-segment citations attached to the ONE grounded text block's text.end, audit M22) + driveAdkTopLevel's `source`/`display.required` emission from groundingChunks/searchEntryPoint.

citationMetadata.note: Gemini Candidate.citationMetadata (source-attribution spans). ALREADY typed on AdkEvent + carried, but was ABSENT from the old @iqai/adk eventField inventory (its LlmResponse did not surface it as a top-level field); @google/adk's LlmResponse declares it, so it now enters the inventory. Folded into driveAdkTopLevel's event-level unmappedEvent provider-raw content.block (index.ts:982-983), alongside branch/customMetadata.

partial.note: isPartial drives the entire streaming/aggregate-suppression state machine (§8.3, the #1 ADK double-render quirk).

turnComplete.note: maybeCloseTurn's hasCompletion check (is_final_response gate, alongside finishReason/errorCode).

errorCode.note: turn.error `code` (when errorMessage is also set) and a mapFinishReason() fallback input; also gates hasCompletion in maybeCloseTurn.

errorMessage.note: turn.error `message` — gates whether a completed turn with errorCode closes via closeTurnError (both set) vs closeTurnDone (errorCode alone, e.g. a non-fatal block reason).

interrupted.note: -> turn.abort{reason:'interrupted'} + marks the turn closed in the facet's own bookkeeping (audit M21, SPEC §8 item 17: interruption terminals are never success).

inputTranscription.note: Speech-to-text transcription of the user's audio input -> a text content.block tagged `_meta:{"agjson/transcription":{role:"input",kind:"transcription"}}` (driveAdkTopLevel index.ts:817-823). Typed on AdkEvent; genuinely read.

outputTranscription.note: Text-to-speech transcription of the model's audio output -> a text content.block tagged `_meta:{"agjson/transcription":{role:"output",kind:"transcription"}}` (driveAdkTopLevel index.ts:824-830). Typed on AdkEvent; genuinely read.

customMetadata.note: Opaque per-event bag -> the event-level unmapped-fields provider-raw content.block (driveAdkTopLevel), alongside branch/citationMetadata (and the facet also still defensively folds candidateIndex, though @google/adk's Event no longer declares it).

usageMetadata.note: mapUsage() -> AgUsage on turn.done (cumulative:false); promptTokenCount/candidatesTokenCount/totalTokenCount/cachedContentTokenCount/thoughtsTokenCount/toolUsePromptTokenCount are the read subfields (the per-modality promptTokensDetails breakdown + serviceTier are NOT read — see packages/e2e/known-acceptable-drops.json's adk entries).

modelVersion.note: NEW on @google/adk's LlmResponse (absent from the old @iqai/adk inventory): 'the model version used to generate the response' (a provenance string, not Live-specific). NOT read or carried by the facet today — a genuine, disclosed gap. Unlike author/timestamp there is no snapshot/nondeterminism blocker (it is optional + absent from every current corpus capture), so folding it into the event-level unmappedEvent provider-raw carry is a clean future enhancement; left un-landed here to keep this migration a manifest-only change (no facet edits), consistent with the ratchet's boundary.

liveSessionResumptionUpdate.note: NEW on @google/adk's LlmResponse: a Live (bidi) connection session-resumption handle (LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate). The facet ingests the NON-live runAsync Event stream, where this never appears (absent from every corpus capture) — connection-lifecycle transport signalling, not a turn-content value on this facet's ingestion boundary. Mirrors the (removed) `error` not-applicable precedent: a field that cannot carry meaningful turn-content JSON here.

goAway.note: NEW on @google/adk's LlmResponse: 'server-side signal that the live connection will be closed soon' (LiveServerGoAway). Live (bidi) connection-lifecycle control, never on the runAsync Event stream the facet serves — same not-applicable rationale as liveSessionResumptionUpdate.

liveSessionId.note: NEW on @google/adk's LlmResponse: 'the session ID of the Live session'. Live (bidi)-only; never present on the runAsync ingestion boundary — same not-applicable rationale as liveSessionResumptionUpdate/goAway.

invocationId.note: turnKey()'s PRIMARY key (the whole-interaction run id / turn correlation key).

author.note: REAL FINDING (fixture-drift ratchet): ALREADY typed on AdkEvent ('user' or the agent name) but NEVER READ anywhere in drive()/driveAdkTopLevel/maybeCloseTurn — a more insidious class of finding than a missing field, since the contract's presence gave a false impression of coverage. INVESTIGATED but DELIBERATELY NOT folded into the generic unmappedEvent carry (unlike branch): on `@google/adk` 1.3.0 `author` is declared OPTIONAL (`author?: string`) on the Event interface, but EMPIRICALLY it is present on EVERY event of the live captures (`author:"spike"` on all events of every packages/e2e adk corpus scenario). Carrying it fired a provider-raw content.block on every native event, breaking the recorded golden `*.agjson.json` snapshots and the cross-framework convergence assertions (adk's sequence gained content.block noise claude/openai's equivalent streams lack) — regenerating those cassette fixtures is outside this ratchet's boundary (facet+manifests+script+SPEC-§8 only). Also triaged reviewed:any in packages/e2e/known-acceptable-drops.json (adk) as a census drop. A more precise, non-noisy home (e.g. an agent-identity field on message.start, for multi-agent transcript attribution) is a plausible FUTURE spec-process enhancement. index.test.ts documents this boundary with an explicit negative-control test.

actions.note: transferToAgent -> handoff{kind:'transfer'}; escalate -> handoff{kind:'escalate'}; requestedAuthConfigs/requestedToolConfirmations -> hitl.ask{kind:'auth'|'approval'} folded into turn.done.outcome.paused.asks[] (audit M26, SPEC §8 items 12+18); stateDelta -> state.delta; artifactDelta/renderUiWidgets/agentState/endOfAgent -> the pre-existing unmappedActions provider-raw carry (driveAdkTopLevel).

longRunningToolIds.note: Read in driveAdkPart to set `longRunning:true` on a matching tool.start.

branch.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet): ALREADY typed on AdkEvent ('multi-agent routing', format agent_1.agent_2.agent_3) but NEVER READ prior to this task — same insidious-finding class as `author`. Folded into the event-level unmappedEvent provider-raw carry. A more precise home (a branch/lineage field alongside handoff/subagent routing) is a plausible future spec-process enhancement. SPEC §8 item 23.

timestamp.note: REAL FINDING (fixture-drift ratchet): ALREADY typed on AdkEvent but NEVER READ anywhere. On `@google/adk` 1.3.0 the Event interface declares `timestamp: number` (REQUIRED, epoch-millis — the AdkEvent contract's own doc still says 'ISO timestamp', a string-vs-number shape divergence noted for a future runtime-adapter fix, out of scope for this name-only ratchet). INVESTIGATED but DELIBERATELY NOT folded into the generic carry, for the SAME reason as `author`: `timestamp` is REQUIRED — present on EVERY event — so carrying it would fire on every native event, the same noise/breakage risk empirically confirmed for `author` (broke the golden snapshots + convergence). Also triaged reviewed:any in packages/e2e/known-acceptable-drops.json (adk) as a census drop. DISCLOSED residual: SPEC.md's conformance item 13 documents a MORE PRECISE existing home for a source timestamp — the bare-key `_meta.timestamp` convention — but attaching it correctly would require choosing WHICH of this one native event's several fanned-out AgEvents anchors the stamp, a design decision beyond this ratchet's mechanical-carry scope. index.test.ts documents this boundary with an explicit negative-control test. Deeper rationale: wall-clock per-event timestamps are the SAME nondeterminism class the AgJSON base envelope deliberately omits (the deleted ts? field, audit M52 / INV-FOLD) — carrying it as content would reintroduce nondeterministic snapshots even if the field were rare.
